data.title: CURRENC regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• CURRENC (NASDAQ:CURR) said it received written notice from Nasdaq stating that the firm has regained compliance with the minimum market value of publicly held shares requirement.
  • Press Release

data.title: PainReform acquires DeepSolar
data.description:
• PainReform (NASDAQ:PRFX) announced on Thursday the successful acquisition of DeepSolar, a provider of AI-driven software solutions designed to optimize solar energy usage in residential and utility-scale solar power plants.
  • This strategic expansion marks an important milestone in PainReform’s long-term diversification strategy, aimed at tapping into high-growth, tech-enabled markets, said the company.
  • Shares of PRFX jumped +49.45% to $2.72 in premarket trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Conduit Pharmaceuticals announces up to $1M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Conduit Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:CDT) on Thursday announced a share repurchase program of up to $1M of its outstanding common stock.
  • The program has no fixed expiration date and may be adjusted or terminated at any time by the company.
  • CDT +14.51% premarket to $0.71.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock index futures drops, yields lower as retail inflation comes in cooler
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Thursday as March CPI came in cooler than expected in March.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -2.3%, and Dow futures (INDU) -1.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 6 basis points to 4.33%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 6 basis points to 3.89%.

The Consumer Price Index came in at -0.1% M/M vs. +0.1% consensus, +2.4% Y/Y vs. +2.6% consensus, and +2.8% prior.

Core CPI came in at +0.1% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, +2.8% Y/Y vs. +3.0% consensus and +3.1% prior

The European Union paused countermeasures on U.S. tariffs.

It was nothing short of a historic day for markets on Wednesday, as U.S. President Donald Trump's pause on reciprocal tariffs against most countries except China sent Wall Street to its best day since the financial crisis of 2008. The S&P 500 rocketed +9.52%; the Nasdaq Composite exploded +12.16%, and the Dow surged +7.87% on Wednesday. 

"Market turmoil turned into elation yesterday as Trump announced a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for countries other than China, which marked a sizable backing off from last week’s tariff escalation," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

Trump on Wednesday said he would issue a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for more than 75 countries who had reached out to the U.S. for trade negotiations.

Perhaps most crucially, we are currently still on course for a disorderly economic decoupling between the world’s two largest economies, with no immediate signs of either the U.S. or China backing down, Sidorov said.

Trump also upped the tariff rate against China to 125%. 

Recession risk in the US is lower than yesterday but remains high, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Initial jobless claims edged up to 223K in the past week.

The Fed balance sheet will also land on Thursday.

data.title: Initial jobless claims rise to 223K in past week, meeting consensus
data.description: Initial jobless claims for the week ended April 5 rose by 4K to 223K, matching the consensus and compared with 219K prior (unrevised), according to data released by the U.S. Department of Labor on Thursday.

data.title: Tivic Health Systems regains compliance with Nasdaq minimum bid price listing rule
data.description:
• Tivic Health Systems (NASDAQ:TIVC) on Thursday said it has received confirmation from Nasdaq that the company has regained compliance with the minimum bid requirements.
  • Consequently, Tivic Health’s common stock will continue to be listed and traded on Nasdaq under the symbol “TIVC.”
  • TIVC -3.73% premarket to $4.64.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CarMax misses lofty Q4 profit expectations
data.description:
CarMax (NYSE:KMX) shares are losing ground in Thursday’s premarket trading as an 81% increase in the company’s profit fell short of Wall Street’s lofty expectations.

“We delivered robust EPS growth driven by increases in unit sales and buys, strong growth in total gross profit, an increase in CAF income, and ongoing management of SG&A,” said Bill Nash, CarMax CEO.

Unfortunately, a profit of $0.58 per share – up from $0.32 per share a year ago – was 8 cents short of expectations and overshadowed a 6.7% improvement in sales to $6B, $30M better than anticipated.

And while sales in the front half of the year were soft, the company made up the difference with strong sales at the back end of the quarter. Accordingly, retail sales were up 6.2%, wholesale sales were up 3.1%, and comparable store used unit sales were up 5.1%.  

The company bought 269K vehicles from consumers and dealers in the quarter, an increase of 15.3%. This breaks down into a 5.3% increase in purchases from consumers and 114.2% gain in sales from dealers.

Online sales accounted for 15% of total retail sales, up a percentage point from a year ago, while revenue from online sales, both retail and wholesale, represented 29% of net revenues, down slightly from 30% in the same quarter last year.

At the same time, the company earned a slightly lower profit per vehicle of $1,045, down 3.8% from a year ago.  

Looking ahead to FY26, CarMax (NYSE:KMX) plans to open 6 new locations and four stand-alone reconditioning/auction centers with capital expenditures to reach $575M.

Shares are down ~8% before Thursday's open, setting up the stock to trade in the red for a fifth time in 6 days.

data.title: Janover acquires ~$4.6M of Solana as part of the new digital asset treasury strategy
data.description:
Commercial property financing company Janover (NASDAQ:JNVR) announced on Thursday the acquisition of ~$4.6M worth of Solana as part of its new digital asset treasury strategy.

Shares were trading +6.68% pre-market at $29.21.

The purchase marks the company's first allocation of capital from its recent $42M financing round.

The board had approved a new treasury policy on April 4, authorizing long-term accumulation of cryptoassets starting with Solana.

data.title: European markets gain as Trump tariff pause sparks rally
Bank7 GAAP EPS of $1.08 beats by $0.08
data.description:
• Bank7 press release (NASDAQ:BSVN): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.08 beats by $0.08.
  • Total assets of $1.8 billion compared to $1.8 billion, an increase of 0.59%
  • Total loans of $1.4 billion compared to $1.4 billion, an increase of 3.63%
  • PPE of $13.7 million compared to $14.9 million, a decrease of 7.86%
  • Total interest income of $30.4 million compared to $33.3 million, a decrease of 8.56%
